duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Registry onlyVaultMaintainer/onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock/onlyVaultGoverner modifiers use OR (||) instead of AND (&&) in revert condition, requiring callers to hold BOTH the vault role AND EMERGENCY_ROLE simultaneously, bricking vault administration
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 9 entries (TECH2-H003 has two instances with same ID) describe the identical boolean logic error in Registry.sol modifiers at lines 34-38. TECH-H001 provides the most precise code comparison to the correctly-implemented onlyVaultGoverner modifier.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Holding position swap-and-pop in Registry.updateHoldingPosition uses calculatorConnector for key reconstruction instead of the original msg.sender, corrupting isPositionUsed mapping for token positions
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same key mismatch between keccak256(msg.sender,...) at creation and keccak256(calculatorConnector,...) during swap-and-pop removal. Note: TECH2-H004 has two distinct entries with same ID; only the 'State Corruption in Holding Position Swap-and-Pop' variant is a duplicate here.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TVL calculation underflow in multiple lending connectors (Silo, Prisma, Compound, Dolomite, Aave) when debt exceeds collateral causes vault-wide DoS via Solidity 0.8.x revert
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same unchecked subtraction pattern (totalCollateral - totalDebt) that reverts when underwater, blocking TVLHelper.getTVL. TECH-H003 provides the most comprehensive listing of all affected connectors with specific line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: UNIv3Connector._getPositionTVL queries pool.positions() using aggregate key (positionManager, tickLower, tickUpper) instead of per-NFT liquidity, overcounting TVL from all users at same tick range
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same aggregate pool position query bug. TECH2-H005 second instance ('UNIv3Connector TVL overcount') is included here. ECON-H001 is selected as representative for its CRITICAL rating and detailed attack economics showing flash-loanable TVL inflation.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SwapAndBridgeHandler onlyExistingRoute modifier uses AND (&&) instead of OR (||), allowing disabled routes with non-zero addresses to remain executable, defeating the emergency disable mechanism
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same boolean logic error in onlyExistingRoute at GenericSwapAndBridgeHandler.sol lines 28-30. TECH2-H006 has two instances with same ID, both removed. TECH-H005 provides the clearest technical analysis with specific condition breakdown.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: DolomiteConnector.openBorrowPosition passes removePosition=true instead of false to registry.updateHoldingPosition, causing borrow positions to be untracked and debt excluded from TVL
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same incorrect boolean flag (true instead of false) in Dolomite.sol openBorrowPosition. TECH2-H010 has two instances with same ID, both removed. TECH-H006 provides the clearest description of the parameter inversion and its no-op behavior.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SiloConnector._getPositionTVL hardcodes 1e18 normalization regardless of token decimals, producing wildly incorrect TVL for non-18-decimal tokens like USDC/USDT
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same hardcoded 1e18 price normalization bug in SiloConnector._getPositionTVL. ECON-H004 provides the best analysis with deployment evidence (USDC as baseToken) and detailed attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SNXv3Connector._getPositionTVL double-counts collateral by adding totalDeposited + totalAssigned, where totalAssigned is already a subset of totalDeposited
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same arithmetic error in SNXConnector._getPositionTVL adding totalDeposited + totalAssigned. TECH-H008 provides the clearest SNX V3 semantics explanation.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity has empty function body, providing zero withdrawal validation when accounting manager extracts tokens from connectors
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same empty function body at Watchers.sol line 8. TECH2-H007 first instance ('Missing Withdrawal Validation') is the duplicate here. TECH-H009 references both affected contracts (Watchers.sol and BaseConnector.sol).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: OmnichainManagerBaseChain.updateTVL lacks monotonic timestamp check, allowing stale cross-chain TVL to overwrite fresher data when LayerZero delivers messages out of order
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the missing updateTime > existingTimestamp check in OmnichainManagerBaseChain.updateTVL. TECH2-H009 second instance ('Cross-chain TVL updates can be replayed/stale') is the duplicate here. ECON-H010 provides the best combination of HIGH severity and economic analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Global registry.flashLoan() address grants onlyManager access to ALL vaults' connectors, enabling cross-vault privilege escalation by any single vault's keeper
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same cross-vault escalation via the global flashLoan address in NoyaGovernanceBase.onlyManager. ECON-H002 provides CRITICAL rating with comprehensive attack economics showing protocol-wide TVL at risk.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BalancerFlashLoan.receiveFlashLoan executes arbitrary calls to unvalidated destinationConnector addresses, allowing keeper to call any contract with any calldata
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe unvalidated destinationConnector[i].call in receiveFlashLoan. TECH2-H008 first instance ('Arbitrary External Calls via Flash Loan Receiver') is the duplicate here. OPS-H011 provides the clearest analysis distinguishing receiver validation from destination validation.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SwapAndBridgeHandler.executeSwap/executeBridge are payable but do not forward msg.value to implementation contracts, permanently trapping native ETH
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe missing {value: msg.value} in the external call from payable executeSwap/executeBridge. TECH2-H009 first instance ('Native ETH Permanently Stuck') and TECH2-H007 second instance ('SwapAndBridgeHandler payable functions may trap') are the duplicates here. ECON2-H007 provides HIGH severity with attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LZHelperSender.updateTVL uses MessagingFee(address(this).balance, 0), spending the entire contract ETH balance as LayerZero fee, enabling economic griefing via ETH donations
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same pattern of using full balance as LZ fee in LZHelperSender.sol. TECH2-H008 second instance ('LZHelperSender.updateTVL may burn all accumulated ETH') is the duplicate here. TECH-H018 provides the clearest analysis of both overspend and donation griefing vectors.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BalancerFlashLoan.makeFlashLoan is publicly callable without access control, enabling griefing via repeated reverting flash loan attempts
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the missing access control on makeFlashLoan and the griefing potential from repeated reverting calls. TECH-H014 provides the most comprehensive analysis including the stateful caller variable risk.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: CamelotConnector._getPositionTVL uses spot getReserves() for LP valuation, vulnerable to flash loan pool manipulation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same spot reserve manipulation in CamelotConnector._getPositionTVL. ECON-H015 provides more detailed attack economics including capital requirements and profit estimates.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Keepers.execute can be griefed via insufficient gasLimit or induced reverts, consuming signing bandwidth and stalling strategy execution
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same operational DoS vector via Keepers.execute with caller-controlled gasLimit and revert behavior. OPS-H014 provides more detailed analysis of the nonce/signing bandwidth implications.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: OmnichainLogic.startBridgeTransaction has no upper bound expiry on approved bridge transactions, allowing stale approvals to be executed indefinitely
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same missing maximum validity window in bridge transaction approval flow. OPS-H017 provides more detailed operational context about the risk of stale approvals under changed conditions.
